title: How Embodied Cognition Affects Vocal Performance: An Integrative Review.

publication.journal: Journal of voice : official journal of the Voice Foundation

assets.abstract: Embodied cognition-the view that cognitive processes are grounded in bodily sensation, action, and environmental interaction-offers a transformative lens for understanding vocal performance. This integrative review synthesizes interdisciplinary research to map key mechanisms, empirical applications, and gaps in the literature on embodied vocal practices. Following Arksey and O'Malley's (2005) methodological framework, we analyzed peer-reviewed studies retrieved from databases such as Web of Scie

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind.
